paper.abstract_en: Flat space holography looks for a dual to quantum gravity in a flat universe, formulated not in the bulk but on its boundary at null infinity I , the surface reached by outgoing light. As the cosmological constant Λ → 0, the boundary of anti-de Sitter space, where the usual AdS/CFT correspondence lives, degenerates onto I , whose geometry carries no notion of time. A field theory there is not only conformal but Carrollian, the structure obtained as the speed of light c → 0, with symmetry the infinite-dimensional BMS4 group of null infinity. We assemble this Carrollian machinery, then ask what ABJM, the Chern-Simons-matter theory of M2-branes and the best-understood field theory dual to AdS4, becomes as c → 0. Of the possible ways to take that limit, only the electric scaling preserves BMS4. Its sextic scalar potential scales away, so a naive reading leaves a free theory. The interaction is not gone, only hidden in the time components Au, Bu of the two gauge fields, which the standard treatment sets to zero. Keeping them, we find they enter without derivatives and integrate out exactly, solving their Gauss-law constraint directly in the action. The result is an effective theory whose interaction, weighted by the inverse matter density M−1, splits into a matter self-coupling, a term tying gauge flux F to matter charge, and a flux-flux term, and forces the two gauge fluxes to coincide, Tr FA = Tr FB . This interacting Carrollian theory is a candidate dual to flat-space gravity from AdS4 × S7.
